LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND - PLAINTIFFS IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO FILE ITS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. CASES SHOULD BE DECIDED ON THE MERITS, AND THE CONSEQUENCES OF A PROCEDURAL FAILURE BY AN ATTORNEY SHOULD NOT BE VISITED ON THE REPRESENTED PARTIES. THE COURT DOES NOT RULE ON DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT RE-FILING ITS DEMURRER ON THE SAME GROUNDS. TODAY'S HEARING SHALL FULFILL DEFENDANT'S MEET AND CONFER REQUIREMENT. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Holly Coulenhan, 415-554-3828 for Defendant/Moving party The City and County of San Francisco.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs fail to state a viable cause of action against the Defendants. Order signed in Open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Attorney David W. Ammons, Deputy City Attorney City and County of San Francisco 415-554-3954, for the Defendant Office of the San Francisco Sheriff the moving party.
No appearance by the opposing party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a cognizable claim in light of the authorities cited by the moving party. Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Reported. (501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Continued To Aug-10-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Continued To Jun-22-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JAMES A. MICHEL, ESQ., Tel No. 415-239-0156 specially appearing for Plaintiffs;
Deputy City Attorney ELIZABETH PEDERSON, ESQ. of City and County of San Francisco Office of the City Attorney, Tel No. 415-554-3800 appearing for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court vacates its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: The Court grants Plaintiffs leave to amend to file its First Amended Complaint. Cases should be decided on the merits, and the consequences of a procedural failure by an attorney should not be visited on the represented parties. The Court does not rule on Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the Complaint. Plaintiffs are granted leave without prejudice to Defendant re-filing its demurrer on the same grounds. Today's hearing shall fulfill Defendant's meet and confer requirement. Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
